steps.title: Count the doubling periods
steps.content: The population doubles every 4 hours. In 20 hours, the number of doubling periods is $20 \div 4 = 5$.

steps.title: Apply exponential growth
steps.content:
When a population doubles $n$ times, it is multiplied by $2^n$. After 5 doublings, the initial population of 250,000 is multiplied by $2^5 = 32$:
$$250,000 \times 2^5 = 250,000 \times 32 = 8,000,000$$
That confirms D is correct.
